technical.note: Pre-mortem #2: CodeMirror 6's async render would race naive assertions. Playwright's web-first assertions with auto-retry are the documented fix.

operational.note: Pre-mortem #3: silent Playwright major-version bump broke auth. Pin + human-acknowledged upgrades.
Pre-mortem #1: flakes accrete until the team disables the gate. Early observability is the countermeasure.

tensions.resolution: 3-shard Playwright matrix on GH Actions (--shard=1/3, 2/3, 3/3). Each shard runs ~8-9 specs, ~2 min wall-clock. Total stays within O2's 5-min budget. Sharding is the canonical 2026 Playwright CI parallelism solution.
validation_criteria: playwright.config.ts reads SHARD_INDEX/SHARD_TOTAL env vars; default totalShards=1 for local runs
p95 of longest-running shard across 10 runs is ≤ 5 min

propagation_effects.note: Shard-level port assignment must be globally unique: worker_port = base_port + (shard_index × max_workers + worker_index)
e2e job becomes a matrix job; a merge/summary step is needed to combine shard test-results into a unified report artifact
Each shard uploads its own artifacts independently; merge-artifacts action needed in summary job
3 shards in parallel, each ~2 min, comfortably within 5-min budget

tensions.resolution: Build-once pattern: a build-binary CI job runs make build and uploads the phronesis binary as a GH Actions artifact. Each e2e shard downloads the artifact (~5-10s) rather than rebuilding (~60-90s). All shards test the same binary.
validation_criteria: ci.yml has a build-binary job with actions/upload-artifact for the phronesis binary
e2e shard jobs have needs: [build-binary] and actions/download-artifact
No shard job invokes make build

propagation_effects.note: build-binary becomes a prerequisite to e2e shards; adds ~90s to critical path but typically hides behind parallel test+lint job
Single binary build ensures all shards test the same binary; eliminates any cross-shard build divergence
Shard startup drops from 90s to 5-10s; budget comfortably met

tensions.resolution: Accept the dependency triangle: O3 (retries=2) masks flakes from B2's gate; B3 (≤5% flake rate) bounds the masking window; O6 (observability) fires before B3 degrades to the point where B2 breaks. The chain is by design. Add structured per-run retry logging (JSON reporter) to make O6's computation more precise.
validation_criteria: playwright.config.ts retries=2 in CI, retries=0 locally
O6 flake-rate weekly job is implemented and files GH Issues at > 5% trailing-20-run rate
release.yml has needs: [e2e] dependency before goreleaser step

propagation_effects.note: O6 is the explicit countermeasure for this dependency; its existence resolves the tension

tensions.resolution: B1 = feature coverage breadth (every shipping feature exercised end-to-end on the configured browser). T2 = browser scope boundary. They operate on orthogonal axes — T2 restricts which browser B1 runs against, not which features are covered. No gap in B1. Optional: post-stabilisation nightly informational Firefox+WebKit job.
validation_criteria: B1 constraint description explicitly notes Chromium-scoped for v1
All specs run in the single configured browser (Chromium); no spec is tagged browser-specific
CI runbook documents optional future nightly Firefox/WebKit job (non-blocking)

propagation_effects.note: Semantics clarified: B1 = feature breadth, T2 = browser breadth; orthogonal axes, no conflict

tensions.resolution: B1's OIDC surface = phronesis handler code (internal/auth/oidc/), not the external OIDC protocol or a real IdP. The stub HMAC verifier exercises the same handler path as a real OIDC token; the difference is in token minting, not handler behavior. Adversarial OIDC cases (expired tokens, invalid sigs) belong in internal/auth/oidc/oidc_test.go (already present). S2 is the correct e2e scope.
validation_criteria: At least one spec sets PHRONESIS_OIDC_ENABLED=1 and mints a token via the stub HMAC path
Spec asserts an OIDC-authenticated session reaches a protected route
No spec attempts a real external OIDC provider network call

propagation_effects.note: OIDC coverage confirmed in-scope via stub path; no gap in B1
Stub usage explicitly justified by B1 semantics

tensions.resolution: O2's p95 is measured on passing (green) runs. A retry storm is incident mode, not p95 mode — and B2 blocks releases during incidents anyway, so wall-clock is irrelevant then. The budget and retry policy are compatible under normal operation (B3 ≤5% flake rate bounds the retry overhead).
validation_criteria: O2 measurement methodology documented in CI runbook: p95 of green runs across trailing 10
O6 fires when trailing-20-run flake rate > 5%, keeping B3 load-bearing

propagation_effects.note: Measurement scope clarified: p95 of passing runs, not all runs
B3 is now load-bearing for TN6's resolution: if flake rate climbs above 5%, O2 becomes at risk under incident conditions

blocking_dependencies.note: Binary build time is the primary wall-clock bottleneck; must be resolved before O2 can be met
Spec count drives shard strategy; B1 scope must be confirmed before O2 architecture is finalised
Gate integrity (B2) is contingent on flake health (B3); O6 is the load-bearing countermeasure

evidence.note: Corrected from m4: formula is in server.ts not playwright.config.ts. Pattern matches: BASE_PORT + shardIndex * MAX_WORKERS_PER_SHARD + workerIndex (line verified in m5).

Negative check passed: waitForSelector absent (only appears in a comment in auth.spec.ts). .toBe() is used only on JSON API response values (status codes, body fields) — not on DOM content. T8 web-first assertions confirmed via toHaveText/toContainText for all editor-content checks.
Verified via e2e-report job which uploads 'playwright-report' artifact after merging blob reports from all 3 shards.
Exact pin: "@playwright/test": "1.50.0" — no caret or tilde.

binding_constraint.reason: The build-binary CI job is the critical path bottleneck. Without it, each of the 3 shards rebuilds from source (~90s × 3 = 4.5 min before a single assertion runs), making O2 impossible to satisfy regardless of spec count or shard architecture. When RT-1 is closed, the full shard matrix is unblocked and all other RTs can proceed in parallel.

tension_validation.reason: Option B includes 3-shard matrix in harness PR; sharding is built in from the start
Option B harness PR includes the build-binary CI job as the first artifact; binding constraint addressed immediately
Release gate (B2 wiring) enabled only in PR 3 when B1 is satisfied; O6 job deferred to PR 4 but planned from the start
All specs in all PRs run in Chromium only; T2 boundary honored throughout
PR 3 includes OIDC stub spec; stub.go already exists (VERIFIED evidence)
O2 measurement on green runs; B3 is load-bearing and O6 job (PR 4) provides the observability

reversibility_log.description: Create tests/e2e/ fixture + spec files (19 files total)
Extend .github/workflows/ci.yml with build-binary + e2e + e2e-report jobs
Wire release.yml needs: [e2e] to block releases on gate
reversibility_log.cost: Removing the gate requires a separate PR; any release attempted while e2e is red is blocked until then

reversibility_log.description: Pin @playwright/test to 1.50.0 (exact, no caret) in frontend/package.json
reversibility_log.cost: Upgrading Playwright requires manual version bump PR that must pass a full e2e run

draft_required_truths.draft_statement: For the e2e suite to cover every currently-shipping surface without drifting, each feature directory under `tests/e2e/` must have at least one spec per capability, and a meta-test must enumerate required directories so a new feature can't be added without a paired spec file.

For e2e to block releases without becoming a productivity tax, the GH Actions job must run in parallel with existing CI jobs (not serial) and complete in ≤5 min p95 — otherwise release wall-clock is dominated by the gate.

For tests to be isolated and production-like simultaneously, each worker must boot its own phronesis binary (built via `make build` once per CI run, reused across workers) against a per-worker `PHRONESIS_PAGES_DIR` tempdir on a unique port.

For auth setup to not flake and for editor timing not to flake, the suite must use (a) a shared storageState fixture that logs in once per worker and (b) Playwright's web-first assertions (`expect(locator).toHaveText(...)`) with default 5s retry rather than manual `waitFor` + `toBe`.

For flake tolerance to not hide real regressions, retries must produce artifacts: trace:'on-first-retry' + screenshot:'only-on-failure' + video:'retain-on-failure', all uploaded as GH Actions artifacts so root-cause analysis is possible without local repro.

For OIDC coverage to work without an external IdP, tests must mint tokens using the existing `internal/auth/oidc/stub.go` HMAC shared-secret path, and the test run must explicitly enable OIDC via `PHRONESIS_OIDC_ENABLED=1` + the stub secret fixture.

For Playwright upgrades not to silently break auth (pre-mortem #3), the version must be pinned in `frontend/package.json` with an exact match (no caret), and `.github/dependabot.yml` or renovate must surface upgrades as human-reviewed PRs that gate on a fresh e2e suite pass.

iterations.result: Generated 19 artifacts in parallel across 6 isolated worktrees (6 groups, ~4x speedup). Group A (CI infrastructure): ci.yml + release.yml + flake-monitor.yml + dependabot.yml — satisfies RT-1⚡ (binding), RT-2, RT-7, RT-9, RT-10. Group B (test infrastructure): playwright.config.ts + 4 fixture files — satisfies RT-4, RT-5, RT-7, RT-8. Group C (auth specs): auth.spec.ts + oidc.spec.ts — satisfies RT-3, RT-6. Group D (wiki/collab specs): crud.spec.ts + sse.spec.ts + crdt.spec.ts — satisfies RT-3. Group E (remaining specs): upload.spec.ts + roles.spec.ts + rate-limit.spec.ts + coverage.spec.ts — satisfies RT-3. Group F (package): frontend/package.json @playwright/test pin — satisfies RT-10. All branches merged to main. Immediate evidence: 9/10 RTs = SATISFIED; RT-4 = PARTIAL (E11 pattern mismatch — wrong file path; intent verified via E10). RT-4 to be corrected in m5. Constraint coverage: 22/22 = 100%.

Derived 10 required truths via backward reasoning from outcome. RT-1 (build-binary CI job) identified as binding constraint — primary wall-clock bottleneck; when closed, unblocks all other RTs. RT-6 is PARTIAL (stub.go VERIFIED, spec PENDING). All others NOT_SATISFIED. Binding dependency chain: RT-1 → RT-2 → {RT-3 through RT-10}. Recommended Option B (harness-first, 4 PRs): PR1=harness+auth specs (RT-1,RT-2,RT-4,RT-5,RT-7,RT-10), PR2=wiki+collab+SSE specs, PR3=blob+admin+rate-limit+OIDC+release-gate-wire (completes RT-3, RT-6; enables B2), PR4=network-interception+flake-monitor (RT-8, RT-9). All 6 tensions validated against Option B: CONFIRMED. Solution options: A=monolithic, B=harness-first (recommended TWO_WAY), C=meta-test-driven.

Found 6 tensions (2 trade_offs, 2 resource_tensions, 2 hidden_dependencies). All 6 resolved. Key decisions: (1) 3-shard Playwright matrix to satisfy B1×O2; (2) build-binary CI job to satisfy T3×O2; (3) O6 accepted as the B2×B3×O3 dependency countermeasure; (4) B1 and T2 confirmed orthogonal (feature breadth vs. browser scope); (5) S2 stub confirmed correct scope for B1 OIDC surface; (6) O2 p95 measurement scoped to green runs. Blocking dependencies for m3: T3→O2 (build pattern), B1→O2 (shard count), B3→B2 (gate integrity). Constraint graph edges added.

Discovered 22 constraints across 5 categories via WebSearch-informed lookup (Playwright 2026 best practices: webServer config, fixture-based auth with storageState, feature-grouped test layout, sharding on CI, network-interception for flake reduction, web-first assertions) plus 4 strategic decisions from user: (1) coverage = every currently-shipping surface (~25 specs), (2) release gate = blocking-on-main with 2 retries, (3) time budget = new O2 ≤5 min p95 on GH Actions, (4) browser = Chromium only for v1. Pre-mortem pass added 3 constraints: T8 (web-first assertions for CodeMirror async render), O5 (Playwright version pin against silent major bumps), O6 (flake-rate observability). Core tactical defaults encoded: test server = production binary via make build (T3) for real-embed coverage; data isolation = per-worker PHRONESIS_PAGES_DIR tempdir (T4); OIDC = HMAC stub via existing internal/auth/oidc/stub.go (S2); failure artifacts = trace + screenshot + video on retry (U2 + O4). 7 draft required truths seeded for m3. Open tensions for m2: B1 (broad scope) × O2 (5min budget) — every-surface at 25 specs may not fit 5min p95 even with sharding; T3 (production binary via make build) × T5 (fast auth via storageState) — binary boot takes ~2s, but only needs to happen once per CI run.

gap_checklist_compliance.note: Data path for e2e tests: Playwright test runner → Chromium → HTTP → phronesis binary → filesystem tempdir. Every transition crosses a process boundary (subprocess for binary, network for HTTP). Covered by T3 (production binary), T4 (per-worker isolation), S3 (ephemeral data).

Auth surfaces tested: password cookie session (S1 fixture creds), OIDC HMAC stub token (S2), API-KEY header. Attack matrix: test framework intentionally uses the canonical happy paths of each — adversarial test coverage (expired tokens, invalid sigs) is deferred to unit tests in internal/auth/oidc/oidc_test.go (already exists). Justification: e2e validates integration; unit tests validate crypto edges.

gap_checklist_compliance.skip_reason: Resource exhaustion concerns are about production runtime behavior. e2e runs against controlled CI environment with explicit test scenarios; DoS-ing the test server is not in scope.

gap_checklist_compliance.note: External dependencies: npm registry (Playwright install), Playwright browser binaries download, GitHub Actions runner pool. Mitigations: O5 (version pin prevents transitive drift), CI cache-dependency-path for Playwright browser binaries (standard Playwright pattern), retries=2 (O3) for transient network flakes. No custom external HTTP calls made by the suite itself.

gap_checklist_compliance.skip_reason: Tests define their own inputs; no external data ingestion.

gap_checklist_compliance.note: Shared state concerns: (a) Playwright workers share Chromium browser binary installation — read-only, no concurrency issue. (b) Per-worker tempdir + unique port covered by T4. (c) storageState (auth fixture) is written once, read many — T5 uses Playwright's documented pattern. (d) CRDT collab test explicitly exercises concurrent writes from two browser contexts; that IS the test.

dependencies.note: B2 gate integrity depends on B3 flake health
B2 uses O3 retries to absorb flakes
B3 monitoring is provided by O6
B1's OIDC coverage is fulfilled by S2's stub path
T3's build requirement is the primary O2 wall-clock bottleneck
B1 spec count drives the sharding strategy that satisfies O2
